items.title: asyncio coroutines and tasks: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Declaring async makes the function run in parallel immediately and every call inside it becomes nonblocking.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Calling async creates a coroutine; schedule or await it, keep blocking work off the event loop, and handle timeout plus cancellation cleanup explicitly.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Calling async creates a coroutine; schedule or await it, keep blocking work off the event loop, and handle timeout plus cancellation cleanup explicitly.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Calling async creates a coroutine; schedule or await it, keep blocking work off the event loop, and handle timeout plus cancellation cleanup explicitly.
Declaring async makes the function run in parallel immediately and every call inside it becomes nonblocking.

answer.explanation: Calling async creates a coroutine; schedule or await it, keep blocking work off the event loop, and handle timeout plus cancellation cleanup explicitly.
